flan.permision.ohmymeteors:allow_meteor_: Allows meteors to spawn inside this claim
message.ohmymeteors.meteor_destroyed.hug: A HUGE meteor has been destroyed!
A HUGE meteor has been destroyed around %s!
message.ohmymeteors.meteor_destroyed.loc: A meteor has been destroyed around %s!
message.ohmymeteors.meteor_shower_spawne: !!! A METEOR SHOWER HAS BEGUN AROUND %s !!!
message.ohmymeteors.meteor_spawned.huge: WATCH OUT! A new HUGE meteor has spawned!
WATCH OUT! A new HUGE meteor has spawned around %s!
message.ohmymeteors.meteor_spawned.local: A new meteor has spawned around %s!
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.lasersSec: The radius in blocks of the xz area covered by the advanced laser block, where meteors will be blown up
How many seconds should this cooldown last?
How many blocks up from the position of the advanced laser should meteors be checked for? (note that the detection box is only 2 blocks thick, not the whole way)
The radius in blocks of the xz area covered by the Basic laser block, where meteors will be blown up
How many seconds should this cooldown last?
How many blocks up from the position of the basic laser should meteors be checked for? (note that the detection box is only 2 blocks thick, not the whole way)
Should the laser be in a cooldown where it can't fire, after it has just fired?
Should the laser be in a cooldown where it can't fire, after it has just fired?
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.meteorBeh: How many chunks should be loaded before the meteors gets discarded? Aka how many loaded chunks are too many? (Each chunk is 16 blocks
A factor to ADD to the speed at which the meteor falls downwards. It is added to a randomly generated number between 1 and 0. Also supports negative numbers (the meteor may go upwards tho!)
A factor to MULTIPLY the speed at which the meteor falls downwards. It is added to a randomly generated number between 1 and 0
Should meteors explode when they come into contact with an entity? (if true you could use an arrow to make the meteor explode for example)
A factor to ADD to the explosion power (by default, the power is equal to the meteor size), thus increasing the damage and radius of the explosion. Also supports negative numbers
A factor to MULTIPLY the explosion power (by default, the power is equal to the meteor size), thus increasing the damage and radius of the explosion.
Should meteors be (more or less) directed towards the nearest player?
Should meteors be able to destroy blocks on impact?
Should meteors spawn the meteor structure after impact?
If true meteors will try to load chunks like enderpearls, hopefully making contact and exploding
Should the meteor structure only replace air blocks?
Should the meteors that come out of a bigger meteor when it's broken be able to destroy blocks on impact?
Should the meteors that come out of a bigger meteor when it's broken be able to destroy spawn structures on impact?
Should the meteors that come out of a bigger meteor when it's broken only replace air blocks for their structure?
Should fire be spawned on meteor impact? (it looks cool!)
Should scatter meteors be spawned when a Basic laser destroys a bigger meteor?
If true will use a spherical explosion instead of the vanilla cubical one. These look nicer at higher explosion power/ranges, but after power 100 become a bit laggy.
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.meteorSho: The maximum number of meteors that are going to spawn in the meteor shower.
The chance for a meteor shower to spawn (on top of the normal meteor spawning chance, so it's meteor_spawn*meteor_shower_spawn)
The delay (in ticks) between each meteor that gets spawned in delayed and direction delayed meteor shower
If true, there will be a chance that meteor showers will spawn (a lot of meteors spawning at the same time & place. It can also sometimes cause brief lag spikes, nothing too dramatic tho)
The minimum number of meteors that are going to spawn in the meteor shower.
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.meteorSpa: Meteor Spawning
A map consisting of biome=chance of spawning. The spawn chance works as described above. The meteors must be able to spawn in those biomes, otherwise they won't!
If set to false will behave like a blacklist, aka meteors won't spawn in those biomes. If true will behave like a whitelist, meteors will spawn ONLY in those biomes.
The same as above but with a possibly different chance at night if enabled
A list of the IDs of the biomes in which meteors can or cannot naturally spawn in (see mode setting below), vanilla or not.
Meteor Spawning

A map consisting of dimension:chance of spawning. The dimension must one in which the meteor can spawn in as specified above, otherwise meteors won't spawn at all. This chance will ALWAYS override the default if present. The spawn chance works as described above.
If set to false will behave like a blacklist, aka meteors won't spawn in those dimensions. If true will behave like a whitelist, meteors will spawn ONLY in those dimensions.
The same as above but with a possibly different chance at night if enabled
The chance for a spawned meteor to be of huge size. Expressed as in 1 in <x> chances. (on top of the 'normal' spawning chance)
The max size limit of how big a huge meteor can be
The maximum size of meteor that can be considered big, and will spawn a big meteor structure upon impact. Only these can spawn a meteor cat by default.
The maximum size of meteor that can be considered medium, and will spawn a medium meteor structure upon impact

Expressed in blocks, represents the max distance (as in a radius) from the origin of the meteor (like a player) in which a meteor can spawn in. (Remember that it has an angled trajectory so it could end up in that area regardless)
The maximum size of meteor that can be considered small, and that will spawn a small meteor structure upon impact
The higher the value the less the meteor will go diagonally, and will keep mostly vertical. (randomness remains so it's not 100%). Generally, you should remain between 1 and 10.
The chance for a meteor to spawn at night if enabled. Expressed as in 1 in <x> chances, as described above.

Expressed as '1 in <x>' chances of spawning a meteor each tick (similar to randomTickSpeed). Setting it to a negative value will disable natural meteor spawn. For example, by default it has a chance of 1 in 30000, so 0.03%, or 1/30000
The world height (y level) at which meteors spawn in the world
The minimum time interval (in seconds) between spawning a meteor and then another

Expressed in blocks, represents the min distance (as in a radius) from the origin of the meteor (like a player) in which the meteor wont' spawn in. (Remember that it has an angled trajectory so it could end up in that area regardless)
Should the spawn rate be different during the night?
The biggest size a natural meteor can have when spawned in. Cannot go above 50.
The smallest size a natural meteor can have when spawned in. Cannot go below 1
Should there be a cooldown between a meteor spawning one meteor and then another?
A list of the IDs of the dimensions in which meteors can or cannot naturally spawn in (see mode setting below), vanilla or not.
Should huge meteors be able to spawn? They are meteors bigger than the maximum size of the big ones

The chance of spawning a special meteor structure in a certain size category (for example the meteor cat meteor), on top of the normal meteor spawn chance. (works like the other chances, aka 1 in <x> probability)
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.notificat: Should the (^above^) announcement be displayed above the hotbar in the actionbar or in chat?
If announcements are enabled, should they also display the (approximate) coordinates of the meteor being spawned/destroyed?
Should players get a message in chat/hotbar when a meteor is destroyed?
Should players get a message in chat/hotbar when a meteor spawns?
Should the explosion sound of the meteor be heard by all players around a certain area from the impact point?
The radius in blocks of the area in which the sound of the meteor will be heard if the option above is true
Should the explosion sound of the meteor be heard by all players online?
Set this to true to have more info while debugging
ohmymeteors.ohmymeteors_config.visualsSe: How far should meteors be rendered. WARNING: YOU NEED TO RESTART YOUR SERVER AND CLIENT IF YOU CHANGE THIS VALUE in order for it to take effect. It is multiplied by the 'entity' render distance of the server
If true, makes the sky glow a certain color when a meteor passes by. This only applies if the meteor is in rendering range and not world or server wide.

The color to apply to the sky when a meteor passes by if it's enabled. By default it's a lightblue-cyan color. Bear in mind that Minecraft still applies its own colors, so some shades (like green) work less well than others (blue)

Chose how the meteor entity projectile looks. 
DYNAMIC_AUTO: The texture goes from normal to hot the closer it gets to ground level or the longer it travels even horizonatally 
DYNAMIC_HEIGHT: The texture goes from normal to hot the closer it gets to ground level 
DYNAMIC_DISTANCE: The texture goes from normal to hot the more it travels 
NORMAL: Fixed texture on the Normal one 
MID: Fixed texture to the Mid level of hotness texture 
HOT: Fixed texture to the Hot level of hotness texture

Note: this setting influences client AND server performance a lot. It is a serverside setting. 
FANCY: the best particle animations, when spawning a lot of meteors can be laggy, 
LESS: a compromise, a bit less lag with still good looking particles ,
MINIMAL: a compromise, a lot less laggy with some particles, 
NONE: most performant, no particles will be sent to clients
The distance in blocks between texture changes from mid to hot when the meteor texture mode is set on DYNAMIC_DISTANCE (or auto using distance)
The distance in blocks between texture changes from normal to mid when the meteor texture mode is set on DYNAMIC_DISTANCE (or auto using distance)

Should meteor and laser particles be forced? They will be rendered further away and look better, but if there are too many of them you may want to disable this for lag reasons. Note: some particles will never displays as forced, like the lasers target box
